summary:
Claim: Thyroxine (T4) has the greatest effect on granulation wound healing among hormones.  
Evidence: - T4 promotes fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis essential for granulation tissue. - It enhances cellular metabolism, supporting overall tissue regeneration.  
Answer: A  

Follow-up insight: In chronic wounds with poor epithelialization, evaluate for hypothyroidism; treating it with levothyroxine may improve epithelial cell activity and wound closure.  

Case study clarification: In an elderly patient with diabetes, hypertension, and fatigue, diabetic complications—not primary adrenal insufficiency—are more likely. Key differences: Primary adrenal insufficiency usually involves hyponatremia and hyperkalemia, whereas diabetes-related weakness stems from hyperglycemia-induced oxidative stress and microvascular damage, often associated with elevated HbA1c and structural heart changes like LVH.  

(Word count: ~490)
